leagueBriefing: The Bundesliga title race took center stage as nine-man Bayern Munich survived a chaotic afternoon at the BayArena to maintain their lead. Borussia Dortmund took full advantage of the leaders' slip, cutting the gap at the top to nine points with a professional home win. Meanwhile, the battle at the bottom remains white-hot as Köln grabbed a vital point on the road and Wolfsburg's winless streak stretched further in a frustrating draw at Hoffenheim.

games.headline: Nine-man Bayern survive BayArena chaos to rescue vital point
games.brief: In a match defined by VAR controversy and disciplinary meltdowns, Bayern Munich showed championship grit to escape Leverkusen with a 1-1 draw. After Aleix García gave the hosts an early lead, Bayern saw two goals disallowed and two players—Nicolas Jackson and Luis Díaz—sent off, yet they still found an equalizer through Díaz before his dismissal to deny Xabi Alonso's men a statement win.
bullets: Aleix García opened the scoring for Leverkusen in the 6th minute with a deflected strike.
Luis Díaz equalized in the 69th minute before being sent off for a second yellow card late in the game.
Nicolas Jackson received a straight red card in the 42nd minute for a challenge on Martin Terrier.
Bayern had two goals disallowed by VAR, including a Harry Kane strike ruled out for offside.
games.context: Bayern remains nine points clear at the top despite the dropped points, while Leverkusen misses a golden chance to ignite their push for the top four.

games.headline: Teenage star Reggiani nets first goal as Dortmund punish Augsburg
games.brief: Borussia Dortmund kept their slim title hopes alive with a dominant 2-0 victory over Augsburg at a raucous Signal Iduna Park. Karim Adeyemi's early clinical finish set the tone, but the afternoon belonged to 18-year-old Luca Reggiani, who marked his first league start with a debut goal to seal the result and keep the pressure on leaders Bayern Munich.
bullets: Karim Adeyemi scored his 8th goal of the season just 13 minutes into the match.
Luca Reggiani (18) became one of the club's youngest scorers with a 59th-minute strike.
Dortmund dominated possession with 68% and struck the woodwork twice in the first half.
Augsburg goalkeeper Finn Dahmen made six saves to prevent a much heavier defeat.
games.context: Dortmund cuts the gap at the top to nine points, ensuring the title race remains mathematically alive for another week.

games.headline: Kalimuendo strike enough for Frankfurt despite late Koch red card
games.brief: Eintracht Frankfurt secured a hard-fought 1-0 win over Heidenheim to solidify their place in the European spots. Arnaud Kalimuendo provided the only goal of the game early in the second half, and despite a late red card for captain Robin Koch that left them clinging on, the Eagles' defense held firm to hand Heidenheim their third straight defeat.
bullets: Arnaud Kalimuendo scored the winner in the 53rd minute, his third goal in four matches.
Robin Koch was sent off for a second bookable offense in the 73rd minute.
Frankfurt held 65% of the possession but struggled to convert 12 total shots into goals.
Heidenheim remains mired in the relegation playoff spot after failing to register a shot on target until late in the second half.
games.context: Frankfurt's win keeps them in a prime position for Champions League qualification, while Heidenheim's survival hopes continue to fade.

games.headline: Prömel late show rescues point for high-flying Hoffenheim
games.brief: Hoffenheim maintained their unbeaten home run in dramatic fashion as Grischa Prömel struck late to cancel out a surprise Wolfsburg opener. The Wolves looked set to end their winless streak after Konstantinos Koulierakis headed them in front, but Hoffenheim's relentless pressure finally told in the 83rd minute to leave both sides frustrated with a share of the points.
bullets: Grischa Prömel saved a point for Hoffenheim with a powerful 83rd-minute equalizer.
Defender Konstantinos Koulierakis scored his first goal for Wolfsburg in the 65th minute.
Wolfsburg has now gone seven matches without a victory, remaining just three points above the drop zone.
Hoffenheim remains in 3rd place but loses ground on the top two following the draw.
games.context: Hoffenheim preserves their top-three status, but the result is a missed opportunity to close in on a stumbling Bayern Munich.

games.headline: El Mala strike rescues vital point for relegation-threatened Köln
games.brief: In a tense battle between two of Germany's traditional giants, 1. FC Köln fought back to earn a precious 1-1 draw at the Volksparkstadion. Fábio Vieira gave Hamburg a deserved lead with a brilliant long-range effort, but Said El Mala responded just before the break to ensure the Billy Goats didn't leave empty-handed in their fight for Bundesliga survival.
bullets: Said El Mala scored the equalizer in the 45th minute, his first goal for Köln.
Fábio Vieira opened the scoring in the 39th minute with a 'dream goal' from 25 yards.
Köln has now gone six games without a win but moves one point further away from automatic relegation.
Hamburg moves up to 10th but remains well adrift of the European qualification spots.
games.context: The draw keeps Köln's survival hopes alive while leaving Hamburg in mid-table obscurity for another week.
